taxonomy: Deiregyne hio is a species of orchid, specifically belonging to the family Orchidaceae. It is a part of the subtribe Spiranthinae and further classified under the genus Deiregyne. The plant is native to the Americas, and its classification is closely related to other species within the same family and subtribe. Further, the species is categorized under the kingdom Plantae and the phylum Magnoliophyta. The detailed classification of Deiregyne hio reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history.

intro: Deiregyne hio, also known as the "Mexican Orchid", is a species of flowering plant in the family Orchidaceae. Deiregyne hio has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and digestive issues, due to its potential health benefits in reducing oxidative stress and promoting immune system function. The bioactive constituents of Deiregyne hio include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which have been reported to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The most common herbal preparations made from Deiregyne hio include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support immune function, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of digestive disorders.
